visa_types.name: FAO Official Visit (Service and Public Affairs Passports)
visa_types.documents_required:
Applicants are no longer required to submit physical photos if they visit the VAC and enrol their biometrics.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport to Denmark Mission. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
An additional copy of your Passport ID page is required to provide to Visa Application Centre for Passport Collection.
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Please read more about the information for FAO Official Visit 
According to the requirement of the Embassy/Consulate of Denmark in China. From 01 November 2019, you will be asked to submit coloured photocopies of all documents (except the passport) in either A4 or legal size at all Visa Application Centres in China. This is requested by the Danish Mission for a better quality of our scanning process after your submission. Please kindly be informed and prepared upon submission.
Note:
- Please note that, when submitting the original notarial certificate, please also provide a copy of the corresponding notarial certificate
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 3 calendar days. (
Notes for submitting missing documents
)
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS Global must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgment. Click here
visa_types.visa_fees:
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210).
Note: From the 03 September 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
VFS Service Fees

[Table:   Family Visit (Incl. Visit To Friends)]
Please note:
- VFS service fee and additional service fees are subject to exchange rate per month, inclusive of VAT, can be paid in mobile payment (Alipay or Wechat Pay) or cash.
- The service fee and
 
additional service (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/dnk/premium-services)
 
are paid in local currency (CNY).
- Please note applicants applying outside Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou (opting for counter collection of passports and documents) need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 4 (CNY amount is as per monthly exchange rate and pay at the time of visa application submission), it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou back to regional VFS Visa Application Centres across China.
visa_types.processing_time: The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your visa  application from the date the mission receives your passport, application and  biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days when  further case processing is needed.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is solely for the holder of Service- and public affairs passports. This visa is given for a travel period of less than 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is solely for the holder of Service- and public affairs passports. This visa is given for a travel period of less than 90 days.

## Visa Fees
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210).
Note: From the 03 September 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
VFS Service Fees

[Table:   Family Visit (Incl. Visit To Friends)]
Please note:
- VFS service fee and additional service fees are subject to exchange rate per month, inclusive of VAT, can be paid in mobile payment (Alipay or Wechat Pay) or cash.
- The service fee and
 
additional service (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/dnk/premium-services)
 
are paid in local currency (CNY).
- Please note applicants applying outside Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou (opting for counter collection of passports and documents) need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 4 (CNY amount is as per monthly exchange rate and pay at the time of visa application submission), it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou back to regional VFS Visa Application Centres across China.

## Documents Required
Applicants are no longer required to submit physical photos if they visit the VAC and enrol their biometrics.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport to Denmark Mission. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
An additional copy of your Passport ID page is required to provide to Visa Application Centre for Passport Collection.
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Please read more about the information for FAO Official Visit 
According to the requirement of the Embassy/Consulate of Denmark in China. From 01 November 2019, you will be asked to submit coloured photocopies of all documents (except the passport) in either A4 or legal size at all Visa Application Centres in China. This is requested by the Danish Mission for a better quality of our scanning process after your submission. Please kindly be informed and prepared upon submission.
Note:
- Please note that, when submitting the original notarial certificate, please also provide a copy of the corresponding notarial certificate
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 3 calendar days. (
Notes for submitting missing documents
)
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS Global must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgment. Click here

## Processing Time
The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your visa  application from the date the mission receives your passport, application and  biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days when  further case processing is needed.

## Photo Specifications
Sample Photograph:
Size of photograph for  visa applications
After the instalment of  a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa  application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm.  Length of the head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of  hair.
The photograph must  still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application  Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa  application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above  format.
Applicants may therefore  obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa  Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information  see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or  visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photograph must be submitted.
Photo Appearance 
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should  be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom  and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not  acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil  that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.

## Visa Application Form
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 03 September  2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee  online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the  cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’  .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form. Please refer to specimen for online application form, For frequently asked questions about online forms, please visit this page (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1445).
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee  online, make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.

visa_types.processing_time:
The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your visa  application from the date the mission receives your passport, application and  biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days when  further case processing is needed.
Citizens of certain countries or categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these cases will be prolonged. Please click See list of these nationalities and categories of persons (annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/visa_code/visa_code_handbook)) for further information.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of visiting family or friends.  This visa is given for a travel period of less than 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of visiting family or friends.  This visa is given for a travel period of less than 90 days.

## Family Members of EU/EEA Nationals Or Swiss Citizens
Family Members of EU/EEA Nationals Or Swiss Citizens
This is solely for family members of EU/EEA nationals or Swiss citizens and is given for a travel period of less than 90 days.
- you are a family member of an European Union (EU) or European Economic Area (EEA) citizen; and
- that EU/EEA citizen is travelling to or is residing in a member state other than that of which he/she is a citizen; and
- you are accompanying the EU/EEA citizen or planning to join him/her in the Schengen State of destination
If you think you are entitled to an accelerated visa procedure you will need to show proof that you meet these criteria when you submit your visa application.
Please note that if you apply under EU/EEA rules and you are not covered, this may impact the processing time and the outcome of your visa application.
Please find more information about Schengen visa according to the EU regulations in this link (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Visa-after-the-EU-rules)

## Visa Fees
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210).
Note: From the 03 September 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
VFS Service Fees

[Table:   Family Visit (Incl. Visit To Friends)]
Please note:
- VFS service fee and additional service fees are subject to exchange rate per month, inclusive of VAT, can be paid in mobile payment (Alipay or Wechat Pay) or cash.
- The service fee and
 
additional service (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/dnk/premium-services)
 
are paid in local currency (CNY).
- Please note applicants applying outside Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou (opting for counter collection of passports and documents) need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 4 (CNY amount is as per monthly exchange rate and pay at the time of visa application submission), it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou back to regional VFS Visa Application Centres across China.

## Documents Required
Applicants are no longer required to submit physical photos if they visit the VAC and enrol their biometrics.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport to Denmark Mission. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
An additional copy of your Passport ID page is required to provide to Visa Application Centre for Passport Collection.
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Please read more about the information of Family/Friends Visit
According to the requirement of the Embassy/Consulate of Denmark in China. From 01 November 2019, you will be asked to submit coloured photocopies of all documents (except the passport) in either A4 or legal size at all Visa Application Centres in China. This is requested by the Danish Mission for a better quality of our scanning process after your submission. Please kindly be informed and prepared upon submission.
Note:

- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 3 calendar days. (
Notes for submitting missing documents
)
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS Global must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgment. Click here

## Processing Time
The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your visa  application from the date the mission receives your passport, application and  biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days when  further case processing is needed.
Citizens of certain countries or categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these cases will be prolonged. Please click See list of these nationalities and categories of persons (annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/visa_code/visa_code_handbook)) for further information.

## Photo Specifications
Sample Photograph:
Size of photograph for visa applications
After  the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs  for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45  mm. Length of the head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top  of hair.
The  photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish  Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform  applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does  not comply with the above format.
Applicants  may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The  Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For  further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa  Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photograph must be submitted.
Photo Appearance 
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should  be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom  and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not  acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil  that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.

## Visa Application Form
From the 03 September 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage).
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form. Please refer to specimen for online application form, For frequently asked questions about online forms, please visit this page (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1445).
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.

- VFS service fee and additional service fees are subject to exchange rate per month, inclusive of VAT, can be paid in mobile payment (Alipay or Wechat Pay) or cash.
- The service fee and
 
additional service (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/dnk/premium-services)
 
are paid in local currency (CNY).
- Please note applicants applying outside Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou (opting for counter collection of passports and documents) need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 4 (CNY amount is as per monthly exchange rate and pay at the time of visa application submission), it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou back to regional VFS Visa Application Centres across China.
visa_types.processing_time: The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your visa  application from the date the mission receives your passport, application and  biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days when  further case processing is needed.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This  visa is solely for the purpose of ADS tourist. This visa is given for a travel  period of less than 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This  visa is solely for the purpose of ADS tourist. This visa is given for a travel  period of less than 90 days.

## Visa Fees
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210).
Note: From the 03 September 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
VFS Service Fees

[Table:   Family Visit (Incl. Visit To Friends)]
Please note:
- VFS service fee and additional service fees are subject to exchange rate per month, inclusive of VAT, can be paid in mobile payment (Alipay or Wechat Pay) or cash.
- The service fee and
 
additional service (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/dnk/premium-services)
 
are paid in local currency (CNY).
- Please note applicants applying outside Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou (opting for counter collection of passports and documents) need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 4 (CNY amount is as per monthly exchange rate and pay at the time of visa application submission), it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou back to regional VFS Visa Application Centres across China.

## Documents Required
Applicants are no longer required to submit physical photos if they visit the VAC and enrol their biometrics.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport to Denmark Mission. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
An additional copy of your Passport ID page is required to provide to Visa Application Centre for Passport Collection.
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Please read more about the information for ADS
According to the requirement of the Embassy/Consulate of Denmark in China. From 01 November 2019, you will be asked to submit coloured photocopies of all documents (except the passport) in either A4 or legal size at all Visa Application Centres in China. This is requested by the Danish Mission for a better quality of our scanning process after your submission. Please kindly be informed and prepared upon submission.
Note:
- Please note that, when submitting the original notarial certificate, please also provide a copy of the corresponding notarial certificate
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 3 calendar days. (
Notes for submitting missing documents
)
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS Global must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgment. Click here

## Processing Time
The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your visa  application from the date the mission receives your passport, application and  biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days when  further case processing is needed.

## Photo Specifications
Sample Photograph:
Size of photograph for  visa applications
After the instalment of  a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa  application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm.  Length of the head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of  hair.
The photograph must  still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application  Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa  application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above  format.
Applicants may therefore  obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa  Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information  see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or  visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photograph must be submitted.
Photo Appearance 
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should  be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom  and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not  acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil  that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.

## Visa Application Form
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 03 September  2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee  online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the  cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’  .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form. Please refer to specimen for online application form, For frequently asked questions about online forms, please visit this page (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1445).
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee  online, make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This  visa is solely for the purpose of tourist. This visa is given for a travel  period of less than 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This  visa is solely for the purpose of tourist. This visa is given for a travel  period of less than 90 days.  

## Family Members of EU/EEA Nationals Or Swiss Citizens
This is solely for family members of EU/EEA nationals or Swiss citizens and is given for a travel period of less than 90 days.
- you are a family member of an European Union (EU) or European Economic Area (EEA) citizen; and that EU/EEA citizen is travelling to or is residing in a member state other than that of which he/she is a citizen; and
- you are accompanying the EU/EEA citizen or planning to join him/her in the Schengen State of destination
If you think you are entitled to an accelerated visa procedure you will need to show proof that you meet these criteria when you submit your visa application.
Please note that if you apply under EU/EEA rules and you are not covered, this may impact the processing time and the outcome of your visa application.
Please find more information about Schengen visa according to the EU regulations in this link (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Visa-after-the-EU-rules)       

## Visa Fees

visa_types.processing_time: Decision within four working days after submission of required documents.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
What is the Red Carpet Program?
The Red Carpet Program introduces simplified procedures for obtaining Danish Schengen visas for applicants who travel regularly to Denmark for business purposes. The aim is to assist those companies, organizations, NGO’s etc. that have business relations with a Danish company or organization.
The Red Carpet Program is open to private and public companies or organizations provided they meet the requirements mentioned on the local Danish embassy’s website. The main one being that the entities have lodged more than 10 visa applications at the Danish Missions in the past year, and have a reasonable number of employees requiring visas for Denmark on an ongoing basis.
What does the Red Carpet Program offer?
- Decision within four working days after submission of required documents
- If fingerprints have been obtained within the last 59 months, there is no need to apply in person.
- Fast track access at the visa centre.
- Hotline and email refer to “
Contact us (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/dnk/contact-vfs)
”
To find further information please visit this website (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa/company-accredition/):
Here you can find further information about the Red Carpet agreement, how you can apply for it, who can apply for it, as well as the terms and conditions regarding the Red Carpet Visa agreement.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
What is the Red Carpet Program?
The Red Carpet Program introduces simplified procedures for obtaining Danish Schengen visas for applicants who travel regularly to Denmark for business purposes. The aim is to assist those companies, organizations, NGO’s etc. that have business relations with a Danish company or organization.
The Red Carpet Program is open to private and public companies or organizations provided they meet the requirements mentioned on the local Danish embassy’s website. The main one being that the entities have lodged more than 10 visa applications at the Danish Missions in the past year, and have a reasonable number of employees requiring visas for Denmark on an ongoing basis.
What does the Red Carpet Program offer?
- Decision within four working days after submission of required documents
- If fingerprints have been obtained within the last 59 months, there is no need to apply in person.
- Fast track access at the visa centre.
- Hotline and email refer to “
Contact us (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/dnk/contact-vfs)
”
To find further information please visit this website (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa/company-accredition/):
Here you can find further information about the Red Carpet agreement, how you can apply for it, who can apply for it, as well as the terms and conditions regarding the Red Carpet Visa agreement.

## Visa Fees
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210).
Note: From the 03 September 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
VFS Service Fees

[Table:   Family Visit (Incl. Visit To Friends)]
Please note:
- VFS service fee and additional service fees are subject to exchange rate per month, inclusive of VAT, can be paid in mobile payment (Alipay or Wechat Pay) or cash.
- The service fee and
 
additional service (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/dnk/premium-services)
 
are paid in local currency (CNY).
- Please note applicants applying outside Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou (opting for counter collection of passports and documents) need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 4 (CNY amount is as per monthly exchange rate and pay at the time of visa application submission), it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou back to regional VFS Visa Application Centres across China.

## Documents Required
Applicants are no longer required to submit physical photos if they visit the VAC and enrol their biometrics.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport to Denmark Mission. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
An additional copy of your Passport ID page is required to provide to Visa Application Centre for Passport Collection.
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Please read more about the information for Red Capet application
According to the requirement of the Embassy/Consulate of Denmark in China. From 01 November 2019, you will be asked to submit coloured photocopies of all documents (except the passport) in either A4 or legal size at all Visa Application Centres in China. This is requested by the Danish Mission for a better quality of our scanning process after your submission. Please kindly be informed and prepared upon submission.
Note:
- Please note that, when submitting the original notarial certificate, please also provide a copy of the corresponding notarial certificate
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 3 calendar days. (
Notes for submitting missing documents
)
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS Global must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgment. Click here

## Processing Time
Decision within four working days after submission of required documents. 

## Photo Specifications
Sample Photograph:
Size of photograph for visa applications
After  the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs  for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45  mm. Length of the head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top  of hair.
The  photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish  Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform  applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does  not comply with the above format.
Applicants  may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The  Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For  further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa  Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photograph must be submitted.
Photo Appearance 
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should  be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom  and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not  acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil  that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.

## Visa Application Form
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 03 September  2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee  online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the  cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’  .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form. Please refer to specimen for online application form, For frequently asked questions about online forms, please visit this page (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1445).
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee  online, make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.

visa_types.name: Greenland and Faroe Island
visa_types.overview:
Greenland and the Faroe Islands do not participate in the Schengen cooperation, a visa to enter Denmark or another Schengen country is not valid for entry into Greenland and/or the Faroe Islands. In practice, an application for a visa for Greenland and/or the Faroe Islands will be accompanied by an application for a Schengen visa. For example, if you plan a trip to Greenland for five days; Norway for four days and Denmark for two days, a visa for entry into Norway is required prior to the application for Greenland and/or the Faroe Islands as you intend to stay in Norway for the longest.
If your main destination in the Schengen area is Denmark, you can to submit a single application for Greenland and/or the Faroe Islands, as well as a Schengen visa. Visa applications for Greenland and the Faroe Islands are issued by Danish missions. Read more about visa to Greenland and the Faroe Islands here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Visa-to-the-Faroe-Island-or-Greenland).
You can apply for visa for Greenland and the Faroe Islands on ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1445)
Please do the following:
- When filling out the application on this website choose Denmark as your main destination
- When you hand in the application cover letter at the visa application center, please inform the staff about your main destination being Greenland and/or the Faroe Islands
Please refer to the visa categories and documents checklists of Denmark Short Term Visas.
visa_types.full_text:
Greenland and the Faroe Islands do not participate in the Schengen cooperation, a visa to enter Denmark or another Schengen country is not valid for entry into Greenland and/or the Faroe Islands. In practice, an application for a visa for Greenland and/or the Faroe Islands will be accompanied by an application for a Schengen visa. For example, if you plan a trip to Greenland for five days; Norway for four days and Denmark for two days, a visa for entry into Norway is required prior to the application for Greenland and/or the Faroe Islands as you intend to stay in Norway for the longest.
If your main destination in the Schengen area is Denmark, you can to submit a single application for Greenland and/or the Faroe Islands, as well as a Schengen visa. Visa applications for Greenland and the Faroe Islands are issued by Danish missions. Read more about visa to Greenland and the Faroe Islands here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Visa-to-the-Faroe-Island-or-Greenland).
You can apply for visa for Greenland and the Faroe Islands on ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1445)
Please do the following:
- When filling out the application on this website choose Denmark as your main destination
- When you hand in the application cover letter at the visa application center, please inform the staff about your main destination being Greenland and/or the Faroe Islands
Please refer to the visa categories and documents checklists of Denmark Short Term Visas.
